hypothesis.statement: We test whether intraday forward behavior conditioned on the known-ahead calendar day-of-week (and the interaction of day-of-week with intraday session position) differs systematically across weekdays. This is a falsifiable seasonality-conditioning hypothesis, not a profitability claim.
hypothesis.economic_rationale: Calendar-driven flow can cluster by weekday for structural reasons (weekly fund-flow cadence, Monday gap absorption, Friday position-squaring into the weekend); whether any of this produces a measurable conditional forward pattern is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve.

lookahead_risk.description: Day-of-week is deterministic from the bar timestamp (the existing day_of_week feature is computed from bar_start_ts.weekday()) and is fully known-ahead, so the conditioner carries no lookahead hazard. The only hazard is treating a holiday-shortened or early-close session as an ordinary weekday; that must be segmented with the existing session-type classification, not a naive weekday rule.
lookahead_risk.mitigation: Use the existing point-in-time day_of_week feature directly; condition jointly on the existing rth_segment_flag / session_id so holiday and early-close sessions are not silently pooled with full sessions. No new metadata surface is required.

expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.statement: A pure calendar position is structurally independent of the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms (regime, vwap_session, bbo_tradability, liquidity_pa) conditioned on. This is the WEAKEST orthogonality claim in the seasonality set because day_of_week is the most generic calendar conditioner and is most at risk of being a proxy for an already-present session-position effect rather than a distinct flow mechanism; the diagnostic, not this statement, decides.
expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.diagnostic_check: Residualize forward outcomes against the dead-mechanism factors (regime/vwap_session/bbo_tradability/liquidity_pa) AND against intraday session position (minutes_from_rth_open) before scoring the day-of-week conditioner, to confirm it is not a relabeling of an intraday-position effect already captured elsewhere.

capacity_turnover_note: Five weekday buckets observed every trading week (roughly 250 sessions/year split across 5 buckets); occurrence count is higher than the calendar-event mechanisms, but the conditional sample is still split across buckets. Pool across the index-futures family per Track-B. No capacity/turnover dollar claim.

data_dependency.detail: Fully covered by the existing SessionFeatureName.DAY_OF_WEEK feature plus existing RTH/session-position features; no new FeatureRequest, no exchange-calendar extension, and no external feed required. Lowest data dependency in the entire differentiated-substrate set.

value_free_status.claims: hypothesis pre-registration only; no alpha/tradability/profitability claim
